title: Workspace Exploration
scenario: Start by identifying the current working directory and listing all files in the active workspace. This helps you orient yourself inside the sandbox environment.
solution: The 'pwd' command prints the current absolute path (which should be /tmp), and 'ls -la' lists all files including hidden ones (like . and ..) in a long listing format.

title: Inspect Running Processes
scenario: Display all active running processes inside the sandbox terminal. Verify that ttyd and the shell (bash) are actively running in your session.
solution: Running 'ps aux' outputs the process table. You will see '/sbin/tini -- ttyd -W bash' running as PID 1, along with the 'ttyd' daemon and 'bash' interactive shell running under your user ID.

title: Verify gVisor Kernel Isolation
scenario: Verify that your container is successfully running inside a gVisor user-space kernel instead of the host kernel. Check the kernel release details.
solution: The output of 'uname -a' should contain 'gvisor'. This indicates that system calls are virtualized by gVisor's sentry kernel rather than passing directly to the host OS.
